jsonLdWeb.name: Stair Stringer Calculator
jsonLdWeb.description: Calculate stair stringer dimensions to building code from total rise. Includes riser height, tread depth, stringer length.

mainEntity.name: What is the building code for stair risers?

acceptedAnswer.text: The IRC (International Residential Code) requires a maximum riser height of 7-3/4 inches (196mm) and a minimum of 4 inches. The maximum variation between risers is 3/8 inch. Most builders target 7 to 7.5 inches for comfortable stairs.

mainEntity.name: How do I calculate the number of stair risers?

acceptedAnswer.text: Divide the total rise (floor to floor height in inches) by the ideal riser height (typically 7 to 7.5 inches). Round to the nearest whole number. Then divide the total rise by that number to get the exact riser height. For example: 108 inches total rise ÷ 7.5 = 14.4, round to 14 risers, each 108/14 = 7.71 inches.

mainEntity.name: What is the 7-11 rule for stairs?

acceptedAnswer.text: The 7-11 rule is a guideline stating that the riser height plus tread depth should equal approximately 17 to 18 inches. With a 7-inch riser, you would use an 11-inch tread (7 + 11 = 18). This produces comfortable, code-compliant stairs. The IRC minimum tread depth is 10 inches.

mainEntity.name: How long should a stair stringer be?

acceptedAnswer.text: Stringer length is calculated using the Pythagorean theorem: stringer = √(total_rise² + total_run²). For example, with a total rise of 108 inches and total run of 130 inches: √(108² + 130²) = √(11664 + 16900) = √28564 = 169 inches or about 14 feet 1 inch.

mainEntity.name: What size lumber do I need for stair stringers?

acceptedAnswer.text: Most stair stringers are cut from 2×12 lumber. After the notches are cut for treads and risers, at least 3.5 inches of solid wood must remain at the thinnest point. A 2×12 (actual 11.25" wide) works for risers up to about 7.75" with 10" treads. For longer spans, consider using 2×14 or engineered lumber.

mainEntity.name: How many stringers do I need?

acceptedAnswer.text: Use a minimum of 3 stringers for stairs up to 36 inches wide. For wider stairs, add a stringer every 16 inches. Stairs 36-48 inches wide need 4 stringers, and stairs wider than 48 inches need 5 or more. Some codes allow 2 stringers for narrow stairs under 30 inches, but 3 is recommended.

howItWorks:
How to Calculate Stair Stringers

Stair stringer calculations ensure your stairs are comfortable and meet building code. The process starts with the total rise — the vertical distance from finished floor to finished floor.

Risers = Total Rise ÷ Ideal Riser Height (round to nearest whole)
  • Step 1: Measure total rise (floor to floor) in inches
  • Step 2: Divide by ideal riser height (7" to 7.5") and round
  • Step 3: Actual riser height = total rise ÷ number of risers
  • Step 4: Number of treads = risers − 1
  • Step 5: Total run = treads × tread depth
  • Step 6: Stringer length = √(total rise² + total run²)
  • Code check: Riser + tread should equal 17–18" for comfort
faqHTML:
Frequently Asked Questions

What is the building code for stair risers?

The IRC requires a maximum riser height of 7-3/4 inches (196mm) and a minimum of 4 inches. The maximum variation between risers is 3/8 inch. Most builders target 7 to 7.5 inches for comfortable stairs.

How do I calculate the number of stair risers?

Divide the total rise (floor-to-floor height in inches) by the ideal riser height (7 to 7.5 inches). Round to the nearest whole number, then divide total rise by that number for the exact riser height. Example: 108" ÷ 7.5 = 14.4, round to 14 risers at 7.71" each.

What is the 7-11 rule for stairs?

The guideline states riser height plus tread depth should equal 17 to 18 inches. A 7" riser with an 11" tread (7+11=18) produces comfortable, code-compliant stairs. IRC minimum tread depth is 10 inches.

How long should a stair stringer be?

Use the Pythagorean theorem: stringer = √(total_rise² + total_run²). For 108" rise and 143" run: √(11664 + 20449) = √32113 ≈ 179.2 inches (about 14 ft 11 in). Buy the next standard lumber length up.

What size lumber for stair stringers?

Most stringers are cut from 2×12 lumber. After notching, at least 3.5" of solid wood must remain. A 2×12 (11.25" actual) works for risers up to ~7.75" with 10" treads. For longer runs, consider 2×14 or engineered lumber.

How many stringers do I need?

Minimum 3 stringers for stairs up to 36" wide. Add one stringer for every additional 16" of width. Stairs 36-48" wide need 4 stringers; wider than 48" need 5+.
